name: The 100 k through Lower Maestrat — Circular from Peñíscola
name_original: Los 100 k por el Bajo Maestrat — Circular desde Peñíscola
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Peníscola / Peñíscola offer a diverse terrain that combines extensive coastline with mountainous landscapes and rural paths. The region features the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, characterized by its dramatic cliffs, Mediterranean flora, and peaks up to 572 meters, providing challenging climbs and scenic routes. Cyclists can also find flat coastal routes along the seafront promenade and dedicated bike lanes connecting to neighboring towns. This varied geography caters to different cycling preferences, from leisurely rides to demanding ascents. Best road cycling routes around Peníscola / Peñíscola The most popular road cycling route is The 100 k through Lower Maestrat — Circular from Peñíscola, a 61.0 miles (98.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 21 minutes to complete, offering a long-distance challenge through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peñíscola – By the Sea loop from Peníscola, a moderate 48.6 miles (78.2 km) path. This route provides extensive coastal views and Mediterranean scenery. Local road cyclists also love the N-340 Highway – Peñíscola loop from Peníscola / Peñíscola, a 20.0 miles (32.2 km) trail leading through coastal roads and rural areas, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Road cycling around Peníscola / Peñíscola is defined by its coastline, the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, and rural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peníscola / Peñíscola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling terrain can I expect in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Peníscola / Peñíscola offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find extensive coastal routes, challenging climbs through mountainous landscapes, and quieter rural paths. The region is characterized by its dramatic cliffs, Mediterranean flora, and peaks up to 572 meters, especially within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park, providing options from leisurely rides to demanding ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Yes, Peníscola / Peñíscola has options for beginner cyclists and families. You can enjoy relaxed, mostly flat coastal routes, such as cycling along the seafront promenade. There's also a dedicated bike lane connecting Peñíscola with the neighboring town of Benicarló, offering an accessible ride through rural landscapes and along the coastline. For an easy loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;N-340 Highway – Peñíscola loop from Peníscola / Peñíscola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For challenging road cycling with significant climbs, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Irta Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; is your primary destination. This area features routes with notable elevation changes and panoramic views. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883391434/the-100-k-through-lower-maestrat-circular-from-peniscola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The 100 k through Lower Maestrat — Circular from Peñíscola&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 960 meters of elevation gain over its 98-kilometer distance.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Road cycling routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola offer stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore the dramatic cliffs and unspoiled coastline of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Irta Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, discover historical remains like Polpis Castle and the Badum Tower, and enjoy views of hidden coves such as Cala Puerto Negro. The historic fortified old town and the imposing Papa Luna Castle also provide a picturesque backdrop to many rides. Additionally, you might encounter beautiful lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3106228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa Llona&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5654440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullal de l'Estany&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes in the Peníscola / Peñíscola area. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883391434/the-100-k-through-lower-maestrat-circular-from-peniscola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The 100 k through Lower Maestrat — Circular from Peñíscola&lt;/a&gt;, offering a long-distance challenge. Another favorite is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23774787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñíscola – By the Sea loop from Peníscola&lt;/a&gt;, which provides extensive coastal views. For a shorter ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;N-340 Highway – Peñíscola loop from Peníscola / Peñíscola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Peníscola / Peñíscola benefits from an excellent Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. The mild temperatures and generally sunny weather, especially outside of the peak summer heat, provide ideal conditions for exploring the region's diverse terrain.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes available around Peníscola / Peñíscola on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes 11 easy routes, 35 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: The road cycling routes in Peníscola / Peñíscola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 19 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which combines stunning coastal views with challenging mountain sections, and the overall scenic beauty of the region, particularly within the Sierra de Irta Natural Park.

Q: Are there any specific services or infrastructure for cyclists in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: Yes, Peníscola / Peñíscola is well-equipped to support cyclists. It's part of the Castellón Cycling network, promoting safe and exciting routes. The town offers various services including rest areas, repair points, and bike-friendly establishments. Some hotels provide specific services like secure bike storage, washing areas, basic workshops, and adapted menus. Bicycle rental is also available in town.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes using public transport in Peníscola / Peñíscola?
A: While the region is well-equipped for cyclists, specific information on public transport access for bikes to trailheads is not detailed. However, Peníscola's central location and dedicated bike lanes connecting to neighboring towns suggest good accessibility within the immediate area. It's advisable to check local transport options for bike carriage if planning to travel further afield by public transport.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, Peníscola / Peñíscola offers routes that can extend for many kilometers. A notable long-distance option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883391434/the-100-k-through-lower-maestrat-circular-from-peniscola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The 100 k through Lower Maestrat — Circular from Peñíscola&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 98 kilometers and takes over four hours to complete, traversing varied landscapes.
tips:
One of the most beautiful and picturesque towns in the Valencian Community and listed among the 100 most beautiful in Spain. Entering the old town is a journey back in time, you can visit the castle located on the rock and have beautiful views along the way. The beaches and the promenade are also well known.
Curious fact, it is an internationally known town after some scenes of Game of Thrones were filmed there.
